question.text: Loss from exchange-traded derivatives can be set off against:

answer.text: Any business income except salary income

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Losses from exchange-traded derivatives (being non-speculative business loss) can be set off against any income head EXCEPT salary. It cannot be set off against salary income. **The Trap:** Salary is the ONLY head against which this loss cannot be set off. **Cross-Exam Context:** This is directly from the workbook sample questions. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Business loss can be set off against income from other sources, capital gains, and other business income — just not salary.
